observed_overlap: Both present alternatives and are treated as information-seeking in recent taxonomy.
decisive_difference: A-not-A uses predicate/modal reduplication plus negation and supplies exactly positive/negative values; `ding6(hai6)` joins independently overt alternatives and can offer more than polarity.

observed_overlap: Both are interrogative clauses and may carry final particles.
decisive_difference: A polar question seeks confirmation of a proposition; the core `ding6` question seeks selection among overt alternatives.

observed_overlap: Copular alternatives may surface as `hai6 A ding6(hai6) B`.
decisive_difference: The first `hai6` is the clause copula; the optional second `hai6` is part of the disjunction marker and neither alone establishes a generic question operator.
